query:
back to ppr.... ran another eval: Done. Results confirmed stable across both runs:

  4,467 scenarios — post-crt-031 (2026-03-29)                    
  
  ┌────────┬────────┬─────────────┬──────────────────────────┐   
  │ Metric │ Value  │    Gate     │          Result          │ 
  ├────────┼────────┼─────────────┼──────────────────────────┤   
  │ CC@5   │ 0.4252 │ ≥ 0.3659    │ PASSED                   │ 
  ├────────┼────────┼─────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ ICD    │ 0.6376 │ ≥ 0.5341    │ PASSED                   │   
  ├────────┼────────┼─────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ MRR    │ 0.005  │ ≥ 0.35      │ nominal FAIL (soft-GT    │   
  │        │        │ (floor)     │ artifact)                │   
  └────────┴────────┴─────────────┴──────────────────────────┘
                                                                 
  New finding this run: Section 5.2 surfaces 5 specific queries  
  where disabling PPR drops MRR from 1.0 to 0.33–0.5 — PPR is
  doing real work at the per-query level for                     
  security/retrospective/HNSW queries, just not visible in     
  aggregate distribution stats. That's actually good news for
  Phase 2.

  Side note: The <task-notification> XML queries polluting the   
  corpus (qlog-1049–1184) are the --retrieval-mode filter that's
  waiting on #326.

entries.title: ADR-005 crt-030: PPR Pipeline Position — Step 6d Between 6b and 6c
background.rs observation fetches need time-window bounds — post-fetch domain filter is a no-op (schema has no source_domain column)
compute_report() repurposed as tick data loader inflates maintenance tick cost with 5 wasted phases
ADR-004 col-031: Activate w_phase_explicit at 0.05; AC-16 Non-Separable from AC-12 (superseded by #3688)
ADR-001: Consolidated Effectiveness Query via Single Store Method (crt-018)
